Proof: work modulo `p`. If `k ≡ 0`, then `k^p ≡ 0 ≡ k`. Otherwise the class of `k` is a unit in
`(ZMod p)ˣ`, whose multiplicative group has order `p - 1`. Hence `k^(p-1) ≡ 1 (mod p)`. Multiplying
by `k` gives `k^p ≡ k (mod p)`, i.e. `k ^ p ≡ k [ZMOD p]`.
